overview.title: API Overview
overview.description: Public entry points for translation, validation, ordering, streaming, inspection, and the supporting type surface.

focusedEntrypoints.description: Full top-level surface for users who prefer one import path.
Type-only entrypoint for the published public type surface.
Clock helpers for creating, parsing, and serializing HLC values.
Pairwise comparison helpers for HLC and causal reasoning, centered on the current primary names rather than deprecated compatibility aliases.
Validation helpers for clocks and event envelopes.
Structured anomaly detection and anomaly-facing runtime types.
Operational inspection helpers for summaries, explanations, and compact result snapshots.
Combined ordering barrel for users who want batch, stream, tie-breakers, and watermarks together.
Bounded batch ordering plus current tie-breaker helpers, without the deprecated compatibility alias kept at the root import.
Raw-record translation surface, including translateBatch() and TranslateBatchPolicyError.

applyTieBreaker.title: applyTieBreaker()
applyTieBreaker.description: Applies the active deterministic tie-breaker when stronger ordering is absent.

compareByCausality.title: compareByCausality()
compareByCausality.description: Performs pairwise causal comparison without inventing evidence.
compareByCausality.summary: Use this for pairwise causal comparison when you need a relation answer without emitting a full ordered batch.
bullets: Possible results are before, after, equal, and unknown.
The current exported pairwise contract does not promote unsupported cross-node cases into a first-class concurrent result.
Supported evidence includes parentEventId, dependencyEventIds, and same-node monotonic sequence.

compareByHlc.title: compareByHlc()
compareByHlc.description: Compares two events by their HLC values only.
signatures: function compareByHlc(a: HlcTimestamp, b: HlcTimestamp): CausalOrdering
compareByHlc.summary: Use this when you want a direct HLC-to-HLC comparison without inferring broader causal semantics.
bullets: Use this when you want direct HLC comparison without broader causal inference.
Possible results are before, after, equal, and unknown for invalid input.

compareDeterministically.title: compareDeterministically()
compareDeterministically.description: Deterministically compares two events when causal proof is insufficient.
compareDeterministically.summary: Use this when stronger causal proof is unavailable but you still need deterministic fallback ordering over event envelopes.
bullets: Use this when stronger causal proof is unavailable but you still need stable fallback ordering.
The return value is a comparator-style number suitable for deterministic ordering decisions.

compareValidatedByCausality.title: compareValidatedByCausality()
compareValidatedByCausality.description: Performs pairwise causal comparison on already validated events.

compareValidatedDeterministically.title: compareValidatedDeterministically()
compareValidatedDeterministically.description: Deterministically compares two already validated events using the current tie-breaker.

inspectOrderBatch.title: inspectOrderBatch()
inspectOrderBatch.description: Builds a payload-agnostic inspection snapshot for one emitted orderEventStream() batch.
inspectOrderBatch.summary: Use this when you want the same compact inspection layer for one emitted orderEventStream() batch, including watermark and correction metadata.
bullets: Preserves emitted watermark, isFinal, and correction metadata in the inspection output.
Summarizes emitted events by orderBasis and confidence.
Helps operators inspect correction-capable stream output without flattening the underlying batch contract.

inspectOrderResult.title: inspectOrderResult()
inspectOrderResult.description: Builds a payload-agnostic inspection snapshot for a bounded orderEvents() result.
inspectOrderResult.summary: Use this when you want a compact operational snapshot of one bounded orderEvents() result without writing custom result-shaping code first.
bullets: Summarizes ordered-event counts by orderBasis and confidence.
Produces an anomaly summary without hiding the original result.anomalies array.
Keeps the output payload-agnostic so it works as a lightweight operator-facing snapshot.

orderEvents.title: orderEvents()
orderEvents.description: Orders a bounded event set and returns ordered output, anomalies, and stats.
orderEvents.summary: This is the main bounded-batch entry point. It validates input, builds ordering constraints from supported causal evidence, and emits a deterministic result even when full proof is not available.
orderEvents.usage:
import { orderEvents } from "causal-order"

const result = orderEvents(events, {
  strict: false,
  detectAnomalies: true,
  tieBreaker: "event_id",
})

console.log(result.ordered)
console.log(result.anomalies)
console.log(result.stats)
bullets: ordered: ordered events with orderIndex, orderBasis, and confidence.
anomalies: invalid, suspicious, or operationally important records.
stats: counts for total, valid, invalid, ordered, and anomaly totals.
tieBreaker: deterministic tie breaker such as event_id or ingestion_order.
strict: the main fail-fast switch for ordering and validation; translation fail-fast remains separately controlled through translateBatch() policy.
detectAnomalies: include emitted anomaly analysis in the returned batch result; turning it off reduces diagnostic output rather than upgrading confidence.
allowUnknownOrder: keep unresolved ordering explicit in non-strict mode; setting it to false strengthens severity posture rather than inventing stronger certainty.
maxClockDriftMs: validation bound for future clock drift checks.

orderEventStream.title: orderEventStream()
orderEventStream.description: Consumes an async event source and emits watermark-aware ordered batches.
signatures:
async function* orderEventStream<T>(
  source: AsyncIterable<EventEnvelope<T>>,
  options?: StreamOrderOptions<T>,
): AsyncIterable<StreamOrderBatch<T>>
orderEventStream.summary: This is the streaming entry point for watermark-aware ordering with explicit late-arrival and correction behavior.
bullets: Consumes an async iterable of event envelopes.
Emits watermark-aware ordered batches.
Supports explicit late-arrival policies and correction-capable output.

orderValidatedEvents.title: orderValidatedEvents()
orderValidatedEvents.description: Orders already validated events without re-running validation.
orderValidatedEvents.summary: Use this when you already hold validated events and want the public ordering step without re-running validation.
bullets: This page shows the supported call shape for already validated events.
Use orderEvents() instead when you want validation included in the same step.

parseHlc.description: Parses a serialized HLC string into its structured parts.
signatures: function parseHlc(value: string): HlcTimestamp

serializeHlc.title: serializeHlc()
serializeHlc.description: Serializes an HLC value into the canonical string form.
signatures: function serializeHlc(clock: HlcTimestamp): string

summarizeEventAnomalies.title: summarizeEventAnomalies()
summarizeEventAnomalies.description: Summarizes emitted event anomalies by type and severity for operator-facing inspection.

summarizeTranslationAnomalies.title: summarizeTranslationAnomalies()
summarizeTranslationAnomalies.description: Summarizes translation anomalies by code, field, mapper, stage, and policy action.

translateBatch.title: translateBatch()
translateBatch.description: Translates raw user-space records into event envelopes and structured translation anomalies.
translateBatch.summary: This is the raw-record ingress entry point. It maps arbitrary user-space records into the event envelope, applies the published timestamp coercion rules, and separates accepted envelopes from structured translation anomalies.
translateBatch.usage:
import { orderEvents, translateBatch } from "causal-order"

const translated = translateBatch(records, {
  getEventId: (record) => record.eventId,
  getNodeId: (record) => record.source,
  getPhysicalTime: (record) => record.occurredAt,
  getSequence: (record) => record.sequence,
  getParentEventId: (record) => record.parent,
  getPayload: (record) => record.body,
})

const ordered = orderEvents(translated.translated, {
  strict: false,
  detectAnomalies: true,
})

console.log(translated.anomalies)
console.log(ordered.ordered)
bullets: translated: readonly translated event envelopes ready for ordering.
anomalies: structured translation failures with field, mapper, stage, and actual-value metadata.
getEventId, getNodeId, and getPhysicalTime are required mappers.
Timestamps accept bigint, safe integer number, and canonical integer string.
Date, ISO timestamp strings, decimals, exponent notation, and unsafe integers are rejected deterministically.
The returned envelope shell is shallowly frozen, while payload remains by reference.

TranslateBatchPolicyError.title: TranslateBatchPolicyError
TranslateBatchPolicyError.description: Error raised when translateBatch() policy configuration chooses fail-fast handling for a structured translation anomaly.
signatures: class TranslateBatchPolicyError<TInput = unknown> extends Error
TranslateBatchPolicyError.summary: This exported error class is raised when translateBatch() policy configuration chooses fail-fast handling for a structured translation anomaly.
bullets: Extends Error and carries the triggering structured anomaly.
Raised only when the configured translation policy chooses fail for the encountered anomaly class.
Available from both causal-order and the narrower causal-order/translate entrypoint.

validateClock.title: validateClock()
validateClock.description: Validates an HLC clock value and reports structured failures or warnings.

validateEvent.title: validateEvent()
validateEvent.description: Validates an event envelope and returns a branded validated value on success.
validateEvent.summary: Use this when you need to validate event-envelope input directly before ordering or other downstream processing.
bullets: valid: true returns a branded value plus any warnings.
valid: false returns structured errors and warnings.
event.id must be a non-empty string.
event.nodeId must be a non-empty string.
event.clock must pass HLC validation.
event.sequence, when present, must be a non-negative bigint.

types.title: Types
types.description: The main public types exported by causal-order.

items.name: NodeId
items.label: NodeId
items.description: Branded node identifier used to separate same-node and cross-node reasoning.

items.name: EventId
items.label: EventId
items.description: Branded event identifier used for duplicate and reference checks.

items.name: HlcTimestamp
items.label: HlcTimestamp
items.description: Serialized hybrid logical clock string.

items.name: ValidatedHlcTimestamp
items.label: ValidatedHlcTimestamp
items.description: Branded HLC timestamp that has passed validation.

items.name: EventEnvelope
items.label: EventEnvelope<T = unknown>
items.description: Public shape for an input event and its ordering metadata.

items.name: ValidatedEventEnvelope
items.label: ValidatedEventEnvelope<T = unknown>
items.description: Validated event envelope safe for downstream ordering logic.

items.name: CausalOrdering
items.label: CausalOrdering
items.description: Pairwise ordering result such as before, after, equal, or unknown.

items.name: CausalEvidence
items.label: CausalEvidence
items.description: Machine-readable evidence explaining why one event was ordered relative to another.

items.name: TranslatedEventEnvelope
items.label: TranslatedEventEnvelope<T = unknown>
items.description: Readonly translated event-envelope shape returned by the raw-record ingress layer.

items.name: TranslateMapper
items.label: TranslateMapper<TInput, TValue>
items.description: Synchronous mapper shape used by translateBatch() field extractors.

items.name: TranslateTimestampInput
items.label: TranslateTimestampInput
items.description: Accepted raw timestamp input for translation: bigint, safe integer number, canonical integer string, or Date for explicit rejection handling.

items.name: TranslateBatchConfig
items.label: TranslateBatchConfig<TInput, TPayload = TInput>

items.name: TranslateBatchPolicy
items.label: TranslateBatchPolicy
items.description: Policy configuration controlling how translation failures are surfaced or failed.

items.name: TranslationAnomalyCode
items.label: TranslationAnomalyCode
items.description: Stable code describing a translation-time mapping or coercion failure.

items.name: TranslationAnomalyDomain
items.label: TranslationAnomalyDomain

items.name: TranslationAnomalyFamily
items.label: TranslationAnomalyFamily
items.description: Stable family split between mapping and structural translation failures.

items.name: TranslationAnomalyCategory
items.label: TranslationAnomalyCategory
items.description: Stable category used for machine-readable translation anomaly handling.

items.name: TranslationRecordFailureAction
items.label: TranslationRecordFailureAction
items.description: Fail-fast or warning action for record-level translation failures.

items.name: TranslationOptionalFieldAction
items.label: TranslationOptionalFieldAction
items.description: Handling action for optional-field translation failures, including continue behavior where omission is honest.

items.name: TranslationPolicyKey
items.label: TranslationPolicyKey
items.description: Stable policy selector describing the translation failure class being handled.

items.name: TranslationPolicyDecision
items.label: TranslationPolicyDecision

items.name: TranslationField
items.label: TranslationField
items.description: Stable field label used in structured translation anomalies.

items.name: TranslationMapperName
items.label: TranslationMapperName
items.description: Stable mapper-function label used in structured translation anomalies.

items.label: TranslationActualValueType

items.label: TranslationDiagnosticSource

items.name: TranslationDiagnosticRecord
items.label: TranslationDiagnosticRecord<TInput = unknown>

items.label: TranslationDiagnosticOrdering

items.name: TranslationFieldReferenceKind

items.description: Stable machine-readable classification attached to translation anomalies.

items.label: TranslationDiagnosticLocation

items.label: EventAnomaly<T = unknown>

items.label: OrderStats

items.name: OrderResult
items.label: OrderResult<T = unknown>
items.description: Top-level bounded ordering result containing ordered events, anomalies, and stats.

items.name: TieBreaker
items.label: TieBreaker<T>

items.name: OrderOptions
items.label: OrderOptions<T>
items.description: Options controlling bounded ordering behavior and anomaly handling.

items.name: OrderedEventExplanation
items.label: OrderedEventExplanation<T = unknown>

items.label: OrderedEventInspection<T = unknown>

items.name: InspectedEventAnomaly

items.name: OrderResultInspection

items.name: OrderBatchInspection
items.label: OrderBatchInspection<T = unknown>

items.name: TranslationAnomalySummary

items.name: PolicyVisibilityKind
items.label: PolicyVisibilityKind

items.name: PolicyVisibilityRecord
